items.q: What is the difference between an IP address and a MAC address?
items.correct: MAC is a hardware identifier (fixed); IP is a logical network address (assignable).
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: IP is for input, MAC is for output.
items.why: Students treat these as synonyms. MAC is burned into the network card (Layer 2); IP is assigned by the network (Layer 3). Both are needed to route packets to a device.

items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: What does in-order traversal of a binary search tree produce?
items.misconception: Nodes in the order they were inserted.
items.why: Students forget the BST property: left subtree less than node less than right subtree. In-order (left, node, right) therefore visits values in sorted order - regardless of insertion order.

items.q: Which technique involves breaking a problem into smaller sub-problems?
items.why: It's Decomposition. “Abstraction” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What is the purpose of a trace table?
items.correct: To track variable values during execution
items.why: It's To track variable values during execution. “To compile code” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many distinct shapes are commonly used in basic flowcharts (start/end, process, decision, input/output)?
items.why: The real figure is 4 shapes. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
